summary: Cuban poet, essayist, and revolutionary. Author of Versos Sencillos (Simple Verses), which provided lyrics for 'Guantanamera.' Wrote 'I Cultivate a White Rose,' 'Our America,' and the dramatic poem Abdala. Killed at the Battle of Dos Ríos fighting for Cuban independence.

works.name: Versos Sencillos / Guantanamera
works.description: Collection of poems including 'A Sincere Man Am I' and 'I Cultivate a White Rose,' adapted into the song Guantanamera.

clues.clue: 'A Sincere Man Am I' — 'from the land where palm trees grow'; adapted by Pete Seeger into 'Guantanamera'
examples: a 'sincere man' 'from the land where the palm trees grow'
this poet whose collection Versos Sencillos provided the lyrics to 'Guantanamera'

clues.clue: 'I Cultivate a White Rose' — given to both 'the true friend' and 'the cruel person' in July as in January
examples: contrasted the 'cruel person' with the 'sincere friend' in a poem about giving the title flower

clues.clue: Prologue says he was 'prescribed rest in the mountains' for 'unjust sorrows'
examples: he was 'prescribed rest in the mountains' to help him cope with 'unjust sorrows'

works.name: The Girl from Guatemala
works.description: Poem about a woman who 'died of love.'

clues.clue: 'Flowered tale' about a girl from Guatemala: 'They say she died of cold / I know she died of love'; inspired by María García Granados
examples: the title woman died: 'They say that she died of cold / But I know that she died of love'

works.name: Our America (Nuestra América)
works.description: Essay arguing against US dominance in Latin America.

clues.clue: Opens 'the conceited villager believes the entire world to be his village'; argues against US values in Latin America; published in Mexican newspaper
examples: proclaimed a 'new America... from the Rio Bravo to the Straits of Magellan'

clues.clue: Killed fighting the Spanish at Battle of Dos Ríos
examples: He was killed fighting the Spanish at the Battle of Dos Rios

clues.clue: Created newspaper Patria; wrote Manifesto of Montecristi; ally of Máximo Gómez
Helped kickstart Modernismo alongside Rubén Darío
examples: Marti helped kickstart this movement of Spanish literature alongside Ruban Dario

clues.clue: Wrote about Guiteau's trial; dramatic poem Abdala about a Nubian king
examples: referred to assassin Charles Guiteau as 'a dry tree in which worms have nested'

comprehensive_summary:
José Martí (1853-1895) was a Cuban poet, essayist, and revolutionary, a key figure in Cuban independence. He was killed fighting the Spanish at the Battle of Dos Ríos.

His Versos Sencillos (Simple Verses) includes poems adapted by Pete Seeger into the song 'Guantanamera.' 'A Sincere Man Am I' describes being 'from the land where palm trees grow.' 'I Cultivate a White Rose' gives the title flower to both 'the true friend' and 'the cruel person' in 'July as in January.' 'The Girl from Guatemala' is a 'flowered tale' about a woman who 'died of love.'

His essay 'Our America' (Nuestra América) argues against US dominance in Latin America. He created the newspaper Patria, wrote the Manifesto of Montecristi, was an ally of Máximo Gómez, and helped kickstart Modernismo alongside Rubén Darío.

Poem: 'I Cultivate a White Rose' — given to both 'the true friend' and 'the cruel person' in 'July as in January'
Poem: 'flowered tale' about the girl from Guatemala — 'They say she died of cold / I know she died of love'
